name: MDB MCP Server
what_it_does: MCP server providing access to malware databases and threat intelligence feeds. Enables security agents to query malware signatures, IOCs (indicators of compromise), malware family information, and threat intelligence data from curated security databases. Built for DFIR analysts and security researchers.
use_cases: Querying malware signatures and IOCs from threat hunting agents
Looking up file hashes against malware databases from triage agents
Correlating threat indicators with malware families from analysis agents
Building malware analysis workflows with threat intelligence context
not_for: Creating or distributing malware (strictly defensive/research use)
Production security monitoring without proper SOC oversight
Non-security use cases — specialized for malware analysis workflows
best_when: A security researcher, malware analyst, or SOC analyst wants AI agents to query malware databases and threat intelligence for IOC lookups, triage, and investigation context.
avoid_when: You need a full SIEM integration or production threat detection pipeline. This is a research and analysis tool — not a real-time detection system.

auth.notes: API key authentication for connected malware databases. Specific requirements depend on which databases are configured.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free open source; connected database APIs may have their own pricing
pricing.paid_tiers: Depends on connected threat intelligence databases
pricing.notes: MCP server is free. Connected databases (VirusTotal, MISP, etc.) may require API subscriptions.

performance.rate_limits: Bounded by connected database rate limits

known_agent_gotchas: DEFENSIVE USE ONLY: Tool provides access to malware data — ensure use is authorized and within legal bounds
Database coverage depends on configuration — verify which databases are connected
Malware hashes submitted to external databases may be logged — consider privacy of analyzed samples
smadi0x86 is a security-focused developer but review code before deploying in production SOC environment
agent_readiness.security_notes: Security research tool. Malware sample metadata may be transmitted to external services during queries. Use in authorized, isolated analysis environments only. Defensive/research use only.

security_score_components.security_notes: Malware database access. Defensive/research only. Sample metadata may be transmitted externally. API keys for connected databases must be secured.
